truthAudit.Summary: Audit of Run 2 findings against the 20 ground truth vulnerabilities. Run 2 correctly identified 6 vulnerabilities (Format String Injection, Command Injection via Config Validation, Stack Buffer Overflow in Input Processing, Hardcoded Credentials, Race Condition in Thread-Pool Counter, and Heap Overflow in Expression Parser) but missed 14 other vulnerabilities including logic bombs, path traversal, weak PRNG, unhandled exceptions, and various concurrency/memory safety issues.

Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the format string vulnerability in log_debug_message where a dynamically sourced format string is passed directly to printf.
Run 2 did not mention the integer overflow in the factorial computation within math_engine::FunctionRegistry.
Run 2 did not identify the use-after-free vulnerability in stage_deferred_history_flush and memory::g_memory_pool.cleanup.
Run 2 correctly identified the command injection vulnerability in ConfigLoader::validate_config where user-controlled configuration values are concatenated and passed to system().
Run 2 correctly identified the stack buffer overflow in SuperCalc::evaluate_expression where strcpy is used without bounds checking for inputs exceeding 1024 bytes.
Run 2 correctly identified the TOCTOU race condition in ThreadSafeCounter::increment where the read of value_ occurs outside the critical section.
Run 2 did not mention the logic bomb in AdminConsole::authenticate that bypasses authentication after 5 failed attempts using EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE.
Run 2 did not identify the path traversal vulnerability in ConfigLoader::load_config where config_dir_ + filename is used without sanitization.
Run 2 correctly identified the heap buffer overflow in ExpressionParser::parse where strcpy is used on a fixed-size memory-pooled buffer without length validation.
Run 2 correctly identified the hardcoded administrative credential in config::security::ADMIN_SECRET.
Run 2 did not mention the weak PRNG in AdminConsole::generate_session_token using std::mt19937 with a limited range.
Run 2 did not identify the thread-unsafe timestamp cache in string_utils::log_debug_message using static std::tm cached_time.
Run 2 did not mention the insecure temporary file handling in AdminConsole::append_override_audit_event using system() to write to temp directories.
Run 2 did not identify the persistent authentication state vulnerability where authenticated_ and session_token_ are not properly cleared on logout.
Run 2 did not mention the unhandled exception in calculator::handle_variable_command when std::stod fails on malformed input.
Run 2 did not identify the integer underflow in memory::MemoryPool::allocate during block splitting when remaining size calculation goes negative.
Run 2 did not mention the unbounded recursion vulnerability in parser::ExpressionParser when parsing deeply nested parentheses.
Run 2 did not identify the data race in ExpressionEvaluator::result_cache_ where cache_mutex_ is not used during concurrent evaluate_cached calls.
Run 2 did not mention the TOCTOU race condition in ConfigLoader::load_config where std::filesystem::exists is checked before opening the file.
Run 2 did not identify the integer division by zero in calculator::calculate_route_score when token_length is zero.

Items.Key: command injection via system() with user input|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|607|609|adminconsole::execute_admin_command|std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str());|an authenticated administrator can execute arbitrary system commands, leading to full system compromise. the 'exec' command prefix allows direct command execution without sanitization.|admin mode activated via 'admin auth sc_ent_2025_auth', then command: admin exec <arbitrary_command>|replace system() with a safe command execution api that validates and whitelists allowed commands, or use a parameterized execution method that prevents shell interpretation.->command injection via system() with user input|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|607|609|adminconsole::execute_admin_command|std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str());|an authenticated administrator can execute arbitrary system commands, leading to full system compromise. the 'exec' command prefix allows direct command execution without sanitization.|admin mode activated via 'admin auth sc_ent_2025_auth', then command: admin exec <arbitrary_command>|replace system() with a safe command execution api that validates and whitelists allowed commands, or use a parameterized execution method that prevents shell interpretation.
